_comment: Phase-2 artifact deck — the architect's validated design output for the trace-column pet, shown full-screen in kitsoki via deck-review. Authored as the validated architecture output that follows the PRD.

scenes.title: Designing the trace-column pet
scenes.subtitle: A self-contained sprite, a dock seam, and an opt-in toggle — zero new state, zero deps
scenes.narration: The architect's validated output: a design for the trace-column pet — a self-contained sprite, a dock seam, and an opt-in toggle, with zero new state and zero dependencies.

scenes.lede: Derive nothing new — dock at the column bottom.
scenes.body: The pet introduces no new application state and reads no trace data. It is a purely decorative component, absolutely positioned at the bottom of the existing trace column, so it overlays the footer instead of joining the row flow. Three small, additive pieces: a sprite component, a dock seam, and an opt-in setting.
scenes.narration: The design derives nothing from new state and reads no trace data. The pet is purely decorative, absolutely positioned at the bottom of the existing trace column so it overlays the footer instead of joining the row flow. Three small additive pieces: a sprite component, a dock seam, and an opt-in setting.

scenes.title: The three slices
scenes.narration: Slice one is the sprite component: TracePet dot vue, inline S-V-G with a C-S-S idle loop. Slice two is the dock seam, anchoring it at the bottom of the trace column without displacing rows. Slice three is the opt-in setting, off by default, that gates whether the pet mounts.

cards.label: Slice 1 · Sprite
cards.sub: TracePet.vue — inline SVG + CSS idle loop (bob, blink, wander, poop, eat); no props, no deps

cards.label: Slice 2 · Dock seam
cards.sub: anchor in InteractiveView.vue .iv__trace; absolutely positioned over the footer, never displaces rows

cards.label: Slice 3 · Opt-in
cards.sub: persisted boolean, default false; 🐾 toggle in the State Diagram header mounts the pet

scenes.title: Data flow
scenes.narration: The data flow is a straight line: the trace-column layout hosts the TracePet, anchored at its bottom, and TracePet renders an inline S-V-G sprite. Nothing flows the other way — the pet is a leaf.

cards.label: → inline SVG sprite
cards.sub: CSS keyframes; decorative leaf, no data in or out

scenes.title: Decisions (ADRs)
scenes.narration: Two anchoring decisions. First: inline S-V-G plus C-S-S keyframes only, so the feature ships zero assets and cannot slow the U-I. Second: opt-in and absolutely positioned at the column bottom, so it can never push, clip, or block a trace row. Reduced-motion is honored throughout.

cells: No images, sprite sheets, libraries, or network — ships zero assets, can't slow the UI
Off by default; overlays the footer so it never pushes, clips, or blocks a trace row
Dampen/pause the bob & blink; pet is decorative (aria-hidden)

scenes.body: A published design: three additive slices, a leaf data flow, and decisions that keep the feature deterministic and weightless — true to kitsoki's no-deps spirit. The validated output the developer decomposes into a sequenced work plan next.
scenes.narration: The validated output: a published design with three additive slices, a leaf data flow, and decisions that keep the feature deterministic and weightless — true to kitsoki's no-dependencies spirit. The developer decomposes this into a sequenced work plan next.
